data.body:
## Description

**What**: End-to-end integration validation that all 10 bridge configuration values are set correctly and the first Jira sync CI run passes after setup.
**Why**: Individual stories configure subsets of values. This story validates that the complete flow works as a unit — no manual post-setup steps remain and the bridge actually functions.
**Scope**:
- IN: Verify all 10 values (ACLI_VERSION, ACLI_SHA256, JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN, BRIDGE_BOT_LOGIN, BRIDGE_BOT_NAME, BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL, BRIDGE_ENV_ID, jira.project) are set after setup; dogfooding run on a net-new project; before/after comparison with manual flow
- OUT: Individual value resolution (covered by Stories 2-5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all 10 Jira bridge configuration values are fully configured after running project-setup with Jira opted in — no manual post-setup steps remain
  Satisfies: "All Jira bridge configuration values used by the CI workflows are fully configured by project-setup"
- When this story is complete, a team member has onboarded a net-new project using only project-setup without consulting bridge documentation, and the first Jira sync CI run passes
  Satisfies: "A team member onboards a net-new project to the Jira bridge using only project-setup, without consulting bridge documentation"
- When this story is complete, the session has been compared against the previous manual setup flow to confirm reduced step count and eliminated expertise requirements
  Satisfies: "The session is compared against the previous manual setup flow to confirm reduced step count"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Requires a real GitHub repository and Jira instance for true end-to-end validation
- [Reliability] Integration of all 5 sub-flows (ACLI, credentials, identity, env ID, fallback) must work as a unit
